text: Present day globalisation and digitalisation presents the Church and historical Christianity with an unparalleled challenge. An unprecedented amount of information and religious material is at the disposal of man. The numerous contemporary heresies are presenting this misinformation in increasingly more complex and sophisticated forms, which are threatening the stability of our society, the unity of the Body of Christ, and the everyday lives of believers. The main goal of this paper is to focus on the role of the Church in maintaining the integrity of the Christian doctrine, while at the same time providing adequate protection for its believers from the influence of various contemporary heresies. We conduct a descriptive qualitative analysis in this paper, which is based on an extensive literature review. In the context of the spiritual reality of heresy, we investigate the challenges faced by the Church in relation to identifying and countering modern heterodoxy, and we clarify the role of the Church in realising its counter-measures in the context of contemporary heresies, and in preparing believers for the purposes of training and protecting them in terms of orthodoxy.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
